seo.title: Building Customer Support with CrewAI + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build customer support with CrewAI + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: CrewAI enables building sophisticated customer support systems where specialized agents collaborate to handle inquiries, retrieve knowledge, and integrate with CRM tools. However, multi-agent delegation can lead to loops, hallucinations, and task drift, compromising response accuracy and compliance. Vex provides critical guardrails to verify agent outputs, filter PII, and ensure policy adherence in high-risk customer support scenarios.

architectureOverview.heading: Customer Support Architecture with CrewAI
architectureOverview.description:
The CrewAI customer support architecture features a triage agent that classifies incoming tickets and delegates to specialized roles: a research agent performs RAG-based knowledge retrieval, a CRM agent handles order lookups and ticket creation via API tools, and a response agent generates polite, compliant replies. Agents communicate via shared memory and task outputs, with tools for vector search, Salesforce/Zendesk integration, and escalation logic.

This setup processes customer queries end-to-end: from intent detection to verified response delivery. Critical failure points include hallucinated CRM data, tone inconsistencies across agents, and unfiltered PII leakage during handoffs.

Vex integrates as task callbacks to validate outputs at each delegation step, ensuring accuracy before proceeding.

risks.name: Agent delegation loops
risks.description: Triage agent endlessly re-delegates to research agent due to vague task completion criteria
risks.mitigation: Vex callback enforces delegation limits and task convergence checks

risks.name: Hallucinated tool outputs
risks.description: CRM agent fabricates order details when API fails, passed to response agent
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies tool outputs against expected schemas and sources

risks.name: Task drift
risks.description: Research agent reinterprets 'find order status' as general product info
risks.mitigation: Vex semantic similarity checks against original task objectives

risks.name: PII leakage in handoffs
risks.description: Customer phone/email numbers passed unfiltered between agents
risks.mitigation: Vex PII detection and redaction before agent delegation

implementation.title: Basic CrewAI Customer Support Crew
implementation.description: Define agents and tasks for triage, research, CRM lookup, and response generation
implementation.code:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI

llm = ChatOpenAI(model='gpt-4o')

triage_agent = Agent(
    role='Ticket Triage Specialist',
    goal='Classify customer intent and delegate appropriately',
    backstory='You expertly categorize support tickets...',
    llm=llm,
    verbose=True
)

research_agent = Agent(
    role='Knowledge Base Researcher',
    goal='Retrieve accurate info from support KB via RAG',
    tools=[rag_tool],  # Assume RAG tool defined
    llm=llm,
    verbose=True
)

crm_agent = Agent(
    role='CRM Integration Specialist',
    goal='Lookup orders and create tickets in CRM',
    tools=[salesforce_tool, zendesk_tool],
    llm=llm,
    verbose=True
)

response_agent = Agent(
    role='Customer Response Writer',
    goal='Write polite, accurate, policy-compliant responses',
    llm=llm,
    verbose=True
)

triage_task = Task(
    description='Analyze: {customer_query} and delegate',
    agent=triage_agent
)

crew = Crew(
    agents=[triage_agent, research_agent, crm_agent, response_agent],
    tasks=[triage_task],
    verbose=2
)

implementation.title: RAG Tool for Knowledge Base
implementation.description: Vector search tool for retrieving support articles
implementation.code:
from crewai_tools import BaseTool
from langchain_community.vectorstores import FAISS
from langchain_openai import OpenAIEmbeddings

class SupportKBTool(BaseTool):
    name: str = "Support Knowledge Base"
    description: str = "Searches support KB for relevant articles"
    
    def _run(self, query: str) -> str:
        # Load FAISS index (pre-built from support docs)
        vectorstore = FAISS.load_local('support_kb_index', 
                                     OpenAIEmbeddings())
        docs = vectorstore.similarity_search(query, k=3)
        return '\n\n'.join([doc.page_content for doc in docs])

rag_tool = SupportKBTool()

implementation.title: CRM Order Lookup Tool
implementation.description: Tool for querying customer orders via CRM API
implementation.code:
from crewai_tools import BaseTool
import requests

class OrderLookupTool(BaseTool):
    name: str = "Order Lookup"
    description: str = "Lookup customer order by email or order_id"
    
    def _run(self, identifier: str) -> str:
        # Mock CRM API call
        response = requests.get(
            f'https://crm.example.com/orders',
            params={'email_or_id': identifier},
            headers={'Authorization': 'Bearer YOUR_API_KEY'}
        )
        if response.status_code == 200:
            orders = response.json()['orders']
            return f"Found {len(orders)} orders: " + \
                   '\n'.join([f"Order {o['id']}: {o['status']}" for o in orders])
        return "No orders found"

crm_tool = OrderLookupTool()

implementation.description: Creates support tickets with customer context

vexIntegration.title: Vex Task Callback for Agent Outputs
vexIntegration.description: Add Vex verification to each agent's task before delegation

vexIntegration.title: PII Redaction Wrapper for CRM Tools
vexIntegration.description: Protect customer data in tool inputs/outputs with Vex
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import VexClient

vex = VexClient(api_key='your-vex-api-key')

def safe_crm_tool_call(tool_func, *args, **kwargs):
    # Redact PII from inputs
    clean_args = vex.redact_pii(
        inputs=args,
        entity_types=['EMAIL', 'PHONE_NUMBER', 'CREDIT_CARD']
    )
    
    # Execute tool
    raw_output = tool_func(*clean_args['clean_inputs'], **kwargs)
    
    # Verify and redact output
    verified = vex.verify(
        content=raw_output,
        checks=[{'type': 'pii_filter', 'action': 'redact'}],
        context={'tool': tool_func.__name__}
    )
    
    return {
        'clean_output': verified['content'],
        'pii_detected': verified['detections'],
        'approved': verified['approved']
    }

# Wrap CRM tools
safe_order_lookup = lambda x: safe_crm_tool_call(OrderLookupTool()._run, x)
safe_ticket_create = lambda e,s: safe_crm_tool_call(TicketCreationTool()._run, e, s)
